title: The Thornton Romances
summary: CME00026 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic selection from James Orchard Halliwell's 1844 Thornton Romances. The encoding retains the edition title page and the four verse texts on printed pp. 1-256, but omits Halliwell's introduction, manuscript descriptions, essay on terminal contractions, notes, and remaining volume matter. Halliwell printed Sir Perceval and Sir Isumbras from Lincoln Cathedral MS 91; Sir Eglamour from Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38 with Lincoln variants; and Sir Degrevant from Cambridge University Library MS Ff.1.6 with Lincoln variants. A complete edition scan and the complete 329-canvas Ff.1.6 digital object are cached. Lincoln exposes one unlabeled selected catalog image and a request route, while no complete public digital Ff.2.38 object was found.
review_notes: The four romances, the 1844 edited selection, the three physical witnesses, the U-M encoding, the checked-in XML, printed facsimiles, and digital reproductions are modeled as distinct layers.

Halliwell's own introduction and editorial essay control the base-text and collation statements. Current Database of Middle English Romance edition notes that call Thornton the base for Eglamour or Degrevant conflict with that primary evidence and are not propagated.

Ff.1.6 has competing foliation systems. The Halliwell/XML and CUDL image labels place Sir Degrevant at ff. 80r-93v (canvases 163-190); the scholarly handlist gives older foliation 96r-109v. Direct inspection of the cached incipit and explicit verifies that these locators refer to one physical text.

Completeness of the Ff.1.6 cache means all 329 canvases in the provider manifest, including covers, inner covers, flyleaves, every numbered leaf, back cover, and spine, at an access derivative profile; it is not a claim to preservation-master resolution or redistribution rights.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00026
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow.
notes: The file is 286,475 bytes and is pinned through the recorded CME submodule commit.
TEMPHEAD records a 2016-05-25 structural and proofing review, not the original creation date of the U-M encoding.

entities.label: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item CME00026
entities.description: A digital edition-level selection containing the 1844 title page and the four romance texts on printed pp. 1-256. It omits the council list, introduction, manuscript descriptions, terminal-contractions essay, notes, and other unencoded volume matter.

date_statements.label: repository XML review
date_statements.notes: A review date for this serialization, not a claimed first-keying or digital-publication date.

entities.label: James Orchard Halliwell, The Thornton Romances (1844)
alternate_labels: The Early English Metrical Romances of Perceval, Isumbras, Eglamour, and Degrevant
entities.description: Halliwell's edited selection of four Middle English romances from three manuscripts at Lincoln and Cambridge, with an introduction, manuscript descriptions, an essay on terminal contractions, textual notes, and additional book matter.

bibliographic.citation: James Orchard Halliwell, ed., The Thornton Romances: The Early English Metrical Romances of Perceval, Isumbras, Eglamour, and Degrevant, Selected from Manuscripts at Lincoln and Cambridge (London: printed for the Camden Society by John Bowyer Nichols and Son, 1844), Camden Society publication no. 30.
bibliographic.title: The Thornton Romances: The Early English Metrical Romances of Perceval, Isumbras, Eglamour, and Degrevant, Selected from Manuscripts at Lincoln and Cambridge
bibliographic.publisher: Printed for the Camden Society by John Bowyer Nichols and Son

notes: CME00026 encodes the title-page content and printed pp. 1-256 only.

Halliwell describes Perceval and Isumbras as taken verbatim from Thornton's manuscript, but his terminal-contractions essay documents expansion and editorial judgment; 'verbatim' is therefore not treated as photographic or graphemically diplomatic reproduction.

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral Library MS 91
entities.description: Robert Thornton's c. 1430-1450 paper miscellany. It is Halliwell's base witness for Perceval and Isumbras and his comparison witness for Eglamour and Degrevant.

holding.notes: Current component loci are Sir Isumbras ff. 109r-114v, Sir Degrevant ff. 130r-138v, Sir Eglamour ff. 138v-147r, and Sir Perceval ff. 161r-176r.

physical_description.extent: 322 leaves, apparently untrimmed, from an original minimum of 340; six paper leaves at front and three at back
physical_description.format: Composite literary, devotional, and medical codex in Robert Thornton's hand; current 1975 S. R. Cockerell binding
notes: The current catalog records damage or text loss at several leaves and fragments at ff. 108, 122, 133A, and 315-321.
The four CME00026 romances occupy separate ranges within a much larger 65-item miscellany.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38
entities.description: A mid-fifteenth-century paper miscellany written by two scribes. Halliwell printed Sir Eglamour from this manuscript and compared the Lincoln copy in his notes.

holding.notes: The current exact scholarly handlist gives Sir Eglamour at ff. 71r-79r; Halliwell and the XML use ff. 63r-71r. No current repository-owned item page resolving that crosswalk was located.

physical_description.extent: At least 261 folios in the current scholarly handlist
physical_description.format: Composite Middle English manuscript written by two scribes
notes: Sir Eglamour is one component among religious, didactic, romance, chronicle, and household texts.

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.1.6
entities.description: The Findern manuscript, a paper household and literary miscellany produced c. 1446-1550 and associated with Findern, Derbyshire. Halliwell used its Sir Degrevant as copy text and collated the defective Lincoln witness.

holding.notes: Sir Degrevant is at CUDL-labeled ff. 80r-93v, canvases 163-190. The scholarly handlist records older foliation ff. 96r-109v.

physical_description.extent: Current digital sequence: covers, inner covers, flyleaves, ff. 1r-159v, rear leaves, back cover, and spine; the manuscript is defective at beginning and end and the handlist reports 29 missing leaves
physical_description.format: Household and literary miscellany associated with Findern, Derbyshire
notes: Direct visual inspection confirms Sir Degrevant begins on CUDL canvas 163 (label 80r) and ends on canvas 190 (label 93v).

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Halliwell's 1844 Thornton Romances
entities.description: A complete 384-page PDF of a University of California Libraries copy, retaining preliminary and final scan matter, the complete lvi + 312-page publication, and all matter omitted from CME00026.

entities.label: Complete Cambridge Digital Library MS Ff.1.6 IIIF facsimile
entities.description: A 329-canvas color digital object from front cover through inner covers, flyleaves, every exposed numbered leaf, rear leaves, back cover, and spine. Sir Degrevant occupies canvases 163-190.
entities.reproduction_format: IIIF Presentation 2 manifest and JPEG access derivatives

entities.label: Lincoln Cathedral catalog selected image for MS 91
entities.description: The one 1071 x 698 JPEG linked from the current MS 91 catalog. The record does not identify its folio, and visual inspection did not establish that it reproduces any CME00026 romance leaf.

entities.label: The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91), Scolar Press facsimile
entities.description: Printed whole-manuscript facsimile with introductions by D. S. Brewer and A. E. B. Owen. A current Wellcome catalog record confirms a 1975 edition; the Lincoln catalog cites the revised 1977 edition.

bibliographic.citation: The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91), introductions by D. S. Brewer and A. E. B. Owen (London: Scolar Press, 1975).
bibliographic.title: The Thornton Manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91)

entities.description: Printed whole-manuscript facsimile with an introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson; no complete openly downloadable copy was verified.

bibliographic.citation: Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson (London: Scolar Press, 1979).

scope.notes: The encoding omits the council list, introduction, manuscript descriptions, terminal-contractions essay, notes beginning on p. 257, and other volume matter.

subject.description: Sir Perceval of Galles and Sir Isumbras

scope.notes: Halliwell calls both texts verbatim from Thornton, subject to the abbreviation-expansion and transcription judgments documented in his editorial essay.
The two folio ranges are retained as an unresolved locator crosswalk for one physical component, not separate witnesses.

object.description: The incomplete Lincoln copy of Sir Eglamour

scope.notes: Direct image inspection confirms the incipit on canvas 163 and explicit on canvas 190.

object.description: The defective Lincoln copy of Sir Degrevant
locators: ff. 130r-138v; lines corresponding to Halliwell 802-1008 are missing

scope.notes: Halliwell says the Lincoln text is linguistically preferable but too defective to use as the continuous copy text.

object.description: Complete 1844 publication and copy-specific scan matter

subject.description: All 329 canvases in the provider manifest

object.description: The complete exposed manuscript object, including covers and spine

subject.description: One catalog JPEG of an unlabeled manuscript page

scope.notes: The image is not mapped to a CME00026 romance portion and is never presented as a complete codex surrogate.

agents.name: Robert Thornton
notes: Scribe and compiler of Lincoln Cathedral MS 91, not author of the four romances.

access.access_method: persistent UMDL handle within the public Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse; the collection also states that bulk XML is available
notes: The persistent handle redirected to an HTTP 403 Cloudflare challenge during anonymous automated checking; the current public collection page remained retrievable and documents public/bulk XML access. This is not treated as permanent human-browser unavailability.

access.access_method: direct complete PDF download and public book viewer

printed texts pp. 1-256 at PDF pages 65-320 / viewer leaves n64-n319
notes: The cached scan includes the entire volume, including council list, introduction, manuscript descriptions, essay on terminal contractions, notes, and final matter omitted from the XML.

access.access_method: one selected JPEG linked from the exact catalog record

local_copies.label: Lincoln Cathedral catalog selected image for MS 91

notes: The catalog supplies no folio label. This privately cached research extract is not redistributable and does not cover a verified CME00026 leaf.

access.access_method: dated check of the exact catalog, its multimedia links, the scholarly handlist and facsimile record, Internet Archive, Open Library, Google Books, and exact-shelfmark web searches
access.format: No complete public digital object verified
notes: The exact catalog exposes only one selected unlabeled JPEG and no IIIF manifest or whole-object download. This dated negative is not proof that no preservation surrogate exists.

access.access_method: Digital images and photocopies may be requested from the Cathedral Library; charges apply and feasibility and terms require confirmation
notes: A complete-object request should name both boards or covers, spine, inside covers and pastedowns, all six front and three rear paper leaves, blanks, all 322 numbered manuscript leaves and fragments, and adjacent works. The four romance portions must then be mapped separately.

access.access_method: Consult the printed facsimile at a holding library; the Wellcome copy is listed on open shelves
Registered users may consult Special Collections; remote researchers may request a virtual reading-room appointment or enquire about ordered images
access.format: Physical manuscript or mediated digital images

access.access_method: dated check of CUDL exact PID patterns and collections, CUL services, Internet Archive, Google Books, the scholarly handlist, and exact-shelfmark web searches
access.format: No complete public digital object verified
notes: The current CUDL paper-stocks collection expressly publishes selected analytical images rather than full facsimiles and did not list Ff.2.38 when checked. No selected Ff.2.38 image was cached.

access.access_method: Locate the out-of-print printed facsimile through a holding library
Cambridge Digital Library viewer, IIIF Presentation manifest, and IIIF Image API services

local_copies.label: Complete Cambridge University Library MS Ff.1.6 IIIF bundle

work_portion.label: Sir Degrevant, Halliwell base witness
notes: Direct inspection verifies the Sir Degrevant incipit on canvas 163 and explicit on canvas 190.
The foliation crosswalk follows the handlist's explicit warning that its contents use an older foliation while CUDL images use the refoliation made for digitization.
Complete provider canvas sequence retained: front cover, inside cover, flyleaves, every numbered leaf, rear leaves, back inside cover, back cover, and spine, including blanks and all adjacent works.

Canvases were requested as non-upscaling IIIF width-1800 derivatives (size segment 1800,). The narrow spine canvas could not supply that derivative without upscaling and was retained at service-native full size; exact successful URLs, sizes, byte counts, and hashes are audited in inventory.json.
The ZIP contains the provider manifest, inventory, and all 329 canvas images.

rights.component: U-M Corpus transcription and markup for CME00026
rights.basis: The current Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page states that its materials are in the public domain in the United States and applies the DPLA No Copyright-United States statement.
notes: This is a provider assertion qualified to the United States, not a worldwide dedication and not a license for manuscript photographs.

rights.component: 1844 printed edition, including Halliwell's editorial matter
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1844 and is the public-domain source class described by U-M for this corpus; this statement is limited to the United States.

rights.component: University of California / Internet Archive scan file and interface assets
rights.basis: The exact PDF is publicly downloadable, but the current item metadata supplies no item-level open license for the digital reproduction as such.

rights.component: Cambridge University Library manuscript photography, downloadable images, and metadata
rights.basis: The exact Presentation manifest states that downloadable images and metadata are licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 3.0 Unported; zooming images are described as copyright Cambridge University Library, all rights reserved.
notes: The cache records a private research copy. Public reuse must comply with attribution and noncommercial limits and any distinction Cambridge makes between downloadable and zooming derivatives.

rights.basis: Lincoln Cathedral's website terms reserve site-material rights, permit downloading extracts for personal reference, prohibit separate use of photographs from accompanying context, and require a license for commercial use.
notes: The ignored cache is a narrow personal-research copy. It is not authorized for corpus redistribution or publication.

rights.basis: The library offers charged digital images on request, but no terms for a not-yet-supplied complete object were available at item level.

rights.component: Prospective Cambridge University Library-supplied Ff.2.38 photography
rights.basis: CUL offers consultation, virtual viewing, and image-order enquiries, but no item-specific license for a not-yet-supplied complete object was found.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Four romances in three larger manuscript miscellanies
editorial_practices.representation: Four consecutively printed verse texts, preceded by editorial preliminaries and followed by notes
editorial_practices.description: Halliwell created an edition-level selection rather than transcribing one unitary medieval work or one physical source.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Incomplete Lincoln copies of Eglamour and Degrevant alongside fuller Cambridge copies
editorial_practices.representation: Cambridge copy texts with Lincoln variants in the notes
editorial_practices.description: Halliwell chose Ff.2.38 for Eglamour and Ff.1.6 for Degrevant, while reporting Lincoln readings rather than constructing one silently mixed text.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Medieval scribal contractions and uncertain terminal strokes
editorial_practices.representation: Expanded roman-type words according to Halliwell's stated contraction readings and grammatical judgments
editorial_practices.description: The essay on terminal contractions documents systematic expansion, uncertainty, and occasional disagreement with manuscript forms.

editorial_practices.representation: Title-page content and printed pp. 1-256 only
editorial_practices.description: The encoding omits precisely the editorial preliminaries and notes needed to identify the source witnesses and editorial method.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Halliwell's square-bracketed supplied and damaged text, italics, page markers, folio markers, and line numbers
editorial_practices.representation: Literal square brackets in line text, italic HI elements, PB elements, and MILESTONE elements; no GAP, UNCLEAR, or NOTE elements
editorial_practices.description: The XML retains 100 square-bracketed content spans as typography rather than structured gap or uncertainty semantics. Its 2016 review corrected and checked those representations but did not reconstruct manuscript readings.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00026.xml, at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: Primary repository evidence for IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID CME00026; the 2016 TEMPHEAD review; title page; four poem divisions; printed-page and manuscript-folio milestones; literal bracketed supplies; and exact repository file provenance. The file is 286,475 bytes, SHA-256 4125f3d33be8a1a0fbbaedd2d8635d22ba33dd894e3dbfe505f3e0a26a229fcc, and Git blob ea42e7830649f1d6d449beebebe41530a982f019.

evidence.citation: XPath and hash inspection of CME00026.xml.
evidence.summary: Reproducibly finds 5 DIV1, 1 DIV2, 446 LG, 6,329 L, 257 PB, 1,308 MILESTONE, 450 HEAD, 15 HI, 2 SUP, 0 GAP, 0 UNCLEAR, and 0 NOTE elements. Part metrics are Perceval 143 LG/2,288 L; Isumbras 68/794; Eglamour 116/1,341; Degrevant 119/1,906.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection page and Middle English Compendium about page.
evidence.summary: Official evidence that the corpus consists largely of manually keyed, proofread TEI-level texts from public-domain editions, offers bulk XML, and applies No Copyright-United States to collection materials. The exact CME00026 handle was challenged by Cloudflare during automated access.

evidence.citation: James Orchard Halliwell, ed., The Thornton Romances (London: Camden Society, 1844), University of California Libraries copy, Internet Archive item thorntonromances00hallrich.
evidence.summary: Complete 384-page scan directly inspected for title page, imprint, extent, contents, printed page boundaries, text openings and endings, notes, and omitted edition matter. The exact cached PDF has SHA-256 3e8468e376e9b94df29c7a777f56df099505f56bde681d5321d4e3587f2d104a and 14,938,077 bytes.

evidence.citation: James Orchard Halliwell, Introduction, Descriptions of the Cambridge and Lincoln Manuscripts, and On the Terminal Contractions, in The Thornton Romances (1844), pp. v-lvi.
evidence.summary: Direct editorial evidence that Perceval and Isumbras were taken from Thornton; Eglamour from Ff.2.38 and Degrevant from Ff.1.6; and the latter two collated against Lincoln. It also documents the incomplete Lincoln texts, Cambridge loci used in 1844, contraction expansion, scribal variation, and Halliwell's reason for avoiding a mixed Degrevant text.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral online catalog, MS 91, The 'Thornton' Romances.
evidence.summary: Responsible holding-repository evidence for current and alternative shelfmarks, c. 1430-1450 date, Robert Thornton's hand, 322-leaf extent, 1975 binding and flyleaves, dimensions, physical losses, four exact romance loci, and one selected multimedia image.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Lincoln, Cathedral Library, MS 91 (Lincoln Thornton Manuscript).
evidence.summary: Current specialist handlist corroborating paper support, North Yorkshire association, dimensions, component loci and line counts, and the 1975 Scolar Press paper facsimile.

evidence.citation: Visual and technical inspection of the selected MS 91 JPEG linked from the Lincoln catalog.
evidence.summary: Confirms a 1071 x 698 color photograph of one manuscript page. The catalog supplies no folio label; the image is therefore left unmapped and partial rather than assigned to a romance by appearance.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Terms of Service Agreement, Intellectual Property Rights.
evidence.summary: Provider terms reserve site-material rights, permit one copy or downloaded extracts for personal reference, prohibit modification and separate use of photographs from accompanying text, require attribution, and require a license for commercial use.

evidence.citation: Lincoln Cathedral, Readers and Researchers, and catalog About the Collections page.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for advance onsite manuscript consultation and charged digital-image or photocopy requests; it does not promise complete-object availability or redistribution permission.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object access check for Lincoln Cathedral MS 91.
evidence.summary: The exact catalog exposes one unlabeled JPEG and no IIIF or complete download. The scholarly handlist and Wellcome record identify printed facsimiles only; exact-shelfmark checks in Internet Archive, Open Library, Google Books, and current web indexes did not yield an obtainable complete digital codex. This is a dated negative, not proof that no institutional surrogate exists.

evidence.citation: Wellcome Collection, The Thornton manuscript (Lincoln Cathedral MS 91), introductions by D. S. Brewer and A. E. B. Owen.
evidence.summary: Current catalog evidence for the 1975 Scolar Press facsimile, London imprint, xxii plus approximately 700 pages, contributors, and an open-shelves physical copy; it does not establish a free online facsimile.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38.
evidence.summary: Exact scholarly-handlist evidence for mid-fifteenth-century date, paper support, dimensions, two scribes, complete contents, Sir Eglamour at ff. 71r-79r, and the 1979 Scolar Press whole-manuscript facsimile. It is not represented as a CUL-owned catalog record.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Use Special Collections, Virtual Reading Room, and Manuscripts Services.
evidence.summary: Official evidence for onsite consultation, remote curator-assisted viewing, and image-order enquiries without claiming that Ff.2.38 already has a public complete digital surrogate.

evidence.citation: Bibliographic record for Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38, introduction by Frances McSparran and P. R. Robinson (Scolar Press, 1979).
evidence.summary: Confirms the printed facsimile's title, contributors, publisher, and date; it does not establish an open digital copy.

evidence.citation: Dated complete-digital-object access check for Cambridge University Library MS Ff.2.38.
evidence.summary: Checks of CUDL's exact PID convention, collection manifests, CUL services, Internet Archive, Google Books, the scholarly handlist, and exact-shelfmark web indexes did not expose a complete public facsimile. CUDL's current paper-stocks collection states that its analytical images are not complete color facsimiles and did not list Ff.2.38.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, Findern Manuscript (Cambridge, University Library, MS Ff.1.6), IIIF Presentation manifest.
evidence.summary: Exact 329-canvas digital-object evidence for identity, holding, current CUDL foliation labels, complete exposed sequence from front cover to spine, IIIF image services, attribution, and CC BY-NC 3.0 downloadable-image and metadata terms.

evidence.citation: Visual inspection of cached Ff.1.6 canvases 1, 163, 190, and 329 and inventory audit of all 329 canvases.
evidence.summary: Confirms full-color photography, front-cover and spine coverage, Sir Degrevant's opening rubric and first stanza on canvas 163 / label 80r, its explicit and closing stanza on canvas 190 / label 93v, and the false result that would have followed from mapping only the handlist's older ff. 96r-109v labels to current canvases.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, Cambridge, University Library, MS Ff.1.6 (Findern).
evidence.summary: Current specialist evidence for c. 1446-1550 date, Findern association, paper support, dimensions, manuscript defects, the paper facsimile, and older foliation ff. 96r-109v for Sir Degrevant. The page explicitly warns that CUDL follows a newer refoliation.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance records for Sir Percevale of Galles, Sir Isumbras, Sir Eglamour of Artois, and Sir Degrevant.
evidence.summary: Current specialist evidence for work identities, IMEV numbers, composition ranges, Northeast Midlands localization, tail-rhyme forms, known manuscript loci, and broad subjects. Its brief modern-edition base-text notes for Halliwell's Eglamour and Degrevant conflict with Halliwell's direct statements and are not used for those lineage edges.

open_questions.question: When and by whom was CME00026 first keyed, and is the checked-in XML byte-identical to U-M's current bulk XML object?
next_steps: Retrieve the exact current U-M XML after the automated Cloudflare challenge is removed or ask U-M for the bitstream.
Compare bytes, XML structure, identifiers, and revision history rather than inferring identity from the handle alone.
Keep the 2016 TEMPHEAD date scoped to repository review.

open_questions.question: Can Lincoln Cathedral supply a complete digital MS 91 object including both covers or boards, spine, pastedowns, all front and rear leaves, blanks, fragments, every manuscript leaf, and adjacent works, and under what private-use and redistribution terms?
next_steps: Ask the Cathedral Library for a complete-object quotation rather than a four-romance excerpt.
Map Perceval ff. 161r-176r, Isumbras ff. 109r-114v, Degrevant ff. 130r-138v, and Eglamour ff. 138v-147r separately after receipt.
Record supplied-file terms before caching or reuse and retain the selected catalog JPEG as a separate partial object.

open_questions.question: Can CUL supply a complete current digital facsimile of Ff.2.38, and how exactly do Halliwell/XML ff. 63r-71r crosswalk to the scholarly handlist's ff. 71r-79r?
next_steps: Ask CUL Special Collections or its imaging service for a complete physical-object surrogate, including covers, spine, endleaves, blanks, all folios, and adjacent works.
Request the current foliation history or inspect the 1979 facsimile and current manuscript directly.
Map Sir Eglamour separately in both systems and do not turn the eight-folio offset into a second witness.

open_questions.question: Which individual Halliwell expansions, emendations, and supplied readings in Perceval, Isumbras, and Eglamour can be verified directly against complete current manuscript images?
next_steps: Obtain complete MS 91 and Ff.2.38 reproductions through the recorded request routes.
Compare Halliwell's square brackets, italics, contraction readings, and note variants against exact leaves.
Keep the present editorial-practice description at the level directly stated in Halliwell's essay until that collation is possible.
